computation_name: Time-Series Matrix Profile and Symbolic Index Search
definition: Use matrix-profile algorithms, symbolic aggregate approximations, iSAX-style indexes, compact dictionaries, or time-series sketches to find nearest subsequences or data-series neighbors before optional exact reranking. This covers shape-based and subsequence audits that are not well represented by a simple DTW lower-bound search.

scale_profile.best_for: Long time-series collections, motif/discord style audits, and subsequence-level SPECTRA where the scientific unit is a window rather than an entire series.
scale_profile.typical_upper_bound: Millions to billions of windows when indexable representations or distributed matrix-profile implementations are used.
scale_profile.avoid_when: The scientific similarity is a simple fixed-length vector distance that can be handled by exact chunked search.

tunable_parameters.name: window_length
tunable_parameters.purpose: Sets the scientific unit and controls subsequence count.

tunable_parameters.name: normalization
tunable_parameters.purpose: Declares whether windows are z-normalized, detrended, aligned, or compared in raw scale.

tunable_parameters.name: rerank_distance
tunable_parameters.purpose: Optional exact distance used after candidate generation.

quality_gates: Report the window definition and whether samples represent full series or subsequences.
Validate approximate candidates against exact window search on a subset.
Check that z-normalization or detrending does not remove the scientific signal being audited.
failure_modes: Changing window length changes the scientific unit of generalization.
Matrix-profile nearest windows can overemphasize local motifs and miss global series differences.
Symbolic discretization can collapse rare but important waveform features.
example_usage: Represent fixed-length windows as vector rows and use dense_vector_topk_similarity.py as an exact reference; production matrix-profile or iSAX systems should emit the same sparse pairwise CSV.

citations.title: Efficient Matrix Profile Computation Using Different Distance Functions
citations.evidence: Develops efficient matrix-profile computation under multiple distance functions for large time-series similarity analysis.

citations.title: Error-bounded Approximate Time Series Joins Using Compact Dictionary Representations of Time Series
citations.evidence: Uses compact dictionary representations for approximate inter-time-series similarity joins with error control.

citations.title: SSH: Sketch, Shingle, and Hash for Massive-Scale Time Series
citations.evidence: Uses sketching, shingling, and hashing to accelerate large-scale time-series similarity search.
